assertions.expectation: Explains that 'No channel' is a legacy configuration that lacks granular maintenance exclusion types
assertions.reason: Response clearly explains that 'No channel' is legacy and specifically describes the lack of granular maintenance exclusion types, particularly highlighting the missing 'no minor or node upgrades' exclusion

assertions.expectation: Mentions that 'No channel' does not support 'no minor or node upgrades' exclusion scope — only the 30-day 'no upgrades' type, AND per-nodepool maintenance exclusion is also limited
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states that 'no minor or node upgrades' exclusion does NOT exist on 'No channel' and that per-nodepool maintenance exclusion is available but limited to 'no upgrades' 30 days

assertions.expectation: Notes that Extended support (24 months) is only available on release channels, not 'No channel'
assertions.reason: Response clearly indicates in the comparison table and text that Extended support (24 months) is available on release channels but not on 'No channel'

assertions.expectation: Notes that rollout sequencing (an advanced feature) is not available on 'No channel'
assertions.reason: Response includes rollout sequencing in the comparison table, showing it's available on release channels but not on 'No channel'

assertions.expectation: Recommends Regular or Stable channel as the closest migration target (legacy behavior matches Stable for minors, Regular for patches)
assertions.reason: Response recommends Regular channel as the primary migration target and mentions Stable as an option for production environments, providing specific migration strategy recommendations

assertions.expectation: Mentions Extended channel as a migration path for customers who want maximum flexibility around EoS enforcement
assertions.reason: Response includes a dedicated section on Extended channel as an alternative migration path, specifically noting it provides maximum flexibility around EoS enforcement with delayed EoS enforcement

assertions.expectation: Warns about exclusion translation: when moving from 'No channel' to release channels, some exclusions don't translate 1:1 and may be ignored — only 'no_upgrades' type translates between the two. Recommends adding a temporary 'no upgrades' exclusion during the transition
assertions.reason: Response does not mention exclusion translation issues or warn that some exclusions may be ignored during migration. It doesn't recommend adding temporary 'no upgrades' exclusions during transition

assertions.expectation: Provides the gcloud command to change the release channel
assertions.reason: Response provides specific gcloud commands to update clusters to different release channels, including 'gcloud container clusters update CLUSTER_NAME --zone ZONE --release-channel regular'
Response does not reference or link to the official Google Cloud documentation comparison table at cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/concepts/release-channels
